theirs:
# notification responsibilities moved to a separate class

class OrderNotifier:
    def notify_order_created(self, order): pass
    def notify_order_cancelled(self, order): pass

class OrderService:
    def __init__(self, notifier):
        self.notifier = notifier

    def create_order(self, user, items):
        order = Order(user=user, items=items)
        order.save()
        self.notifier.notify_order_created(order)
        return order

    def cancel_order(self, order_id):
        order = Order.get(order_id)
        order.status = 'cancelled'
        order.save()
        self.notifier.notify_order_cancelled(order)
        return order
description: THEIRS refactored: notifications extracted into OrderNotifier class. OURS extended OrderService inline. Heuristic classifier may classify as semantic due to overlapping OrderService definitions. A trained ML classifier should outperform the heuristic. That is the motivation for training on your organization's own conflict history.
